title: Ensure That Retention Policies on Cloud Storage Buckets  Used for Exporting Logs Are Configured Using Bucket Lock

input_row.title: Ensure That Retention Policies on Cloud Storage Buckets  Used for Exporting Logs Are Configured Using Bucket Lock
input_row.description: Enabling retention policies on log buckets will protect logs stored in cloud storage buckets from being overwritten or accidentally deleted. It is recommended to set up retention policies and configure Bucket Lock on all storage buckets that are used as log sinks.
input_row.rationale: Logs can be exported by creating one or more sinks that include a log filter and a destination. As Cloud Logging receives new log entries, they are compared against each sink. If a log entry matches a sink's filter, then a copy of the log entry is written to the destination. Sinks can be configured to export logs in storage buckets. It is recommended to configure a data retention policy for these cloud storage buckets and to lock the data retention policy; thus permanently preventing the policy from being reduced or removed. This way, if the system is ever compromised by an attacker or a malicious insider who wants to cover their tracks, the activity logs are definitely preserved for forensics and security investigations.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Open the Cloud Storage browser in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser. 2. In the Column display options menu, make sure Retention policy is checked. 3. In the list of buckets, the retention period of each bucket is found in the Retention policy column. If the retention policy is locked, an image of a lock appears directly to the left of the retention period. From Google Cloud CLI 1. To list all sinks destined to storage buckets: gcloud logging sinks list --folder=FOLDER_ID | --organization=ORGANIZATION_ID | --project=PROJECT_ID 2. For every storage bucket listed above, verify that retention policies and Bucket Lock are enabled: gsutil retention get gs://BUCKET_NAME For more information, see https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/using-bucket- lock#view-policy.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. If sinks are not configured, first follow the instructions in the recommendation: Ensure that sinks are configured for all Log entries. 2. For each storage bucket configured as a sink, go to the Cloud Storage browser at https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser/. 3. Select the Bucket Lock tab near the top of the page. 4. In the Retention policy entry, click the Add Duration link. The Set a retention policy dialog box appears. 5. Enter the desired length of time for the retention period and click Save policy. 6. Set the Lock status for this retention policy to Locked. From Google Cloud CLI 1. To list all sinks destined to storage buckets: gcloud logging sinks list --folder=FOLDER_ID | --organization=ORGANIZATION_ID | --project=PROJECT_ID 2. For each storage bucket listed above, set a retention policy and lock it: gsutil retention set [TIME_DURATION] gs://[BUCKET_NAME] gsutil retention lock gs://[BUCKET_NAME] For more information, visit https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/using-bucket-lock#set- policy.
input_row.impact: Locking a bucket is an irreversible action. Once you lock a bucket, you cannot remove the retention policy from the bucket or decrease the retention period for the policy. You will then have to wait for the retention period for all items within the bucket before you can delete them, and then the bucket.
input_row.default_value: By default, storage buckets used as log sinks do not have retention policies and Bucket Lock configured.
input_row.additional_information: Caution: Locking a retention policy is an irreversible action. Once locked, you must delete the entire bucket in order to "remove" the bucket's retention policy. However, before you can delete the bucket, you must be able to delete all the objects in the bucket, which itself is only possible if all the objects have reached the retention period set by the retention policy.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control enables verification through GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) as indicated in the audit section which specifies using gcloud and gsutil commands. The rationale emphasizes technical aspects of log retention and protection, which are standard service properties and can be queried through APIs. The control also has clear pass/fail criteria as indicated in the remediation section about the retention settings and Bucket Lock status. This all lends itself to an automated audit approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_storage_bucket_retention_policy_locked

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the following:

1. **Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) Command:**
   - To list all logging sinks that redirect logs to Cloud Storage buckets:
     ```
     gcloud logging sinks list --folder=FOLDER_ID | --organization=ORGANIZATION_ID | --project=PROJECT_ID
     ```
   
2. **Use GCP CLI Command:**
   - For each sink destination (i.e., Cloud Storage bucket), verify the retention policies and Bucket Lock status:
     ```
     gsutil retention get gs://[BUCKET_NAME]
     ```

3. **Enumerate Specific Resources:**
   - Iterate through the list of sinks previously retrieved to get the associated GCS buckets.

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Check the return of `gsutil retention get gs://[BUCKET_NAME]`. The response should confirm:
     - The retention duration is set (not `None` or a blank).
     - The Bucket Lock status is enabled (there should be a lock status indicating it is locked).

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** 
     - The response includes a retention period that is a defined time (e.g., "30 days") and indicates that the retention policy is locked.
   - **Fail:** 
     - The retention period is `None`, not defined, or indicates that the retention policy is not locked.

This automated check ensures compliance with the control for cloud storage buckets used for logging, enhancing forensics and security investigations by enforcing log retention policies effectively.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be verified using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. The use of `gcloud logging sinks list` and `gsutil retention get` commands allows for automated checks of the retention policies and Bucket Lock status on Cloud Storage buckets. These tasks are well-suited for automation given the clear pass/fail criteria and the ability to query necessary information programmatically.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The use of `gcloud logging sinks list` to identify log sinks and `gsutil retention get` to check retention policies and Bucket Lock status is valid.
- However, the use of "GCP CLI Command" is a bit misleading as it should be referred to as "gsutil command" since gsutil is a separate tool from the gcloud SDK.
- The program name `gcp_storage_bucket_retention_policy_locked` is descriptive but could be more specific to indicate that it is checking for log export buckets specifically.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Program Name:** Consider renaming the program to `gcp_logging_export_bucket_retention_policy_locked` to better reflect its specific focus on log export buckets.
- **Additional Checks:** Ensure that the script also verifies that the retention period meets organizational policy requirements (e.g., minimum retention duration).
- **Error Handling:** Implement error handling in the script to manage cases where buckets do not exist or permissions are insufficient.
- **Documentation:** Include comments or documentation within the script to clarify each step and its purpose.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded based on the capabilities of the Google Cloud SDK and the gsutil tool. The approach aligns with best practices for automating compliance checks using available GCP tools.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Modify the program name to `gcp_logging_export_bucket_retention_policy_locked` for clarity.
- Enhance the script to include checks for compliance with organizational retention duration policies and improve error handling.
- Ensure the documentation within the script is comprehensive to aid in understanding and future maintenance.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the control can be automated using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK commands. The tasks involved, such as checking retention policies and Bucket Lock status, are well-suited for automation due to the availability of specific commands (`gcloud logging sinks list` and `gsutil retention get`) that can programmatically verify the required settings. The clear pass/fail criteria further support an automated approach.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_export_bucket_retention_policy_locked

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Cloud Storage Buckets
- API/CLI Method: 
  - `gcloud logging sinks list` to identify log sinks
  - `gsutil retention get gs://[BUCKET_NAME]` to check retention policies and Bucket Lock status
- Properties to Check: 
  - Retention period is set and not `None`
  - Bucket Lock status is enabled
- Pass Criteria: 
  - Retention period is specified and meets organizational policy requirements
  - Bucket Lock is enabled, indicated by a lock icon or status
- Fail Criteria: 
  - Retention period is not set or is `None`
  - Bucket Lock is not enabled

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both concurred on the automated approach, and the second opinion provided a suggestion to refine the program name for clarity, which has been incorporated.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
